label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to find the number of nonnegative integer solutions to the equation 2x + 3y + 7z = 50. Hmm, nonnegative integers mean x, y, z can be 0, 1, 2, etc. Let me think about how to approach this. First, maybe I can fix one variable and then solve for the others. Let me try fixing z first because 7 is the largest coefficient, maybe that will reduce the number of cases. For each possible value of z, I can find the number of solutions for x and y. Let me write the equation as 2x + 3y = 50 - 7z. Since x and y are nonnegative integers, 50 - 7z must be nonnegative. So 50 - 7z ≥ 0 → z ≤ 50/7 ≈ 7.14. So z can be 0, 1, 2, ...,
